title: Minimal grounded-vs-blank-slate + safety kill-gate (a few fixtures)
context: Before investing in HIGH-tier content (M2), the project requires an early go/no-go that validates the source-grounded+cited approach clearly beats blank-slate generation on accuracy, groundedness, and safety. This minimal kill-gate at M1 runs against a small number of fixture topics, providing a lightweight thesis check before the more comprehensive full eval in M4 (nutrition-during-treatment-eval-013). Depends on nutrition-during-treatment-pipeline-004.
objective: Implement a minimal evaluation harness that runs a couple of fixture topics through the source-grounded+cited pipeline versus a blank-slate baseline, reports accuracy/groundedness/readability and a basic safety check, and asserts that the grounded+cited approach clearly beats the baseline. If the kill-gate fails, HIGH-tier M2 content investment pauses for review before proceeding.
acceptanceCriteria: Eval harness implemented: runs at least two fixture topics through source-grounded+cited pipeline and through a blank-slate baseline; compares accuracy, groundedness/citation coverage, readability (grade level), and a basic safety check
Safety check asserts the grounded+cited path does not produce personalized targets, dosing, interaction judgments, or pro-misinformation output on the fixture inputs

Kill-gate condition documented and enforced: if source-grounded+cited+safe does not clearly beat blank-slate on the fixture set, a CI signal (exit code, flag, or explicit FAIL output) halts M2 content investment pending review
Eval results (per fixture, aggregate delta) printed to stdout in a human-readable format; harness runs in pnpm test or via a dedicated pnpm eval script
Reviewed and signed off by the project maintainer and an oncology RDN/CSO (for validation of the fixture content and safety check scope); sign-off recorded
output: A merged PR implementing the minimal eval harness with grounded-vs-blank-slate comparison and safety kill-gate on a small fixture set, plus documentation of the go/no-go rule that gates M2 content investment.
